name: Valerian Extract Standardized
summary: Standardized valerian extract has mixed evidence for sleep quality and should remain limited/inconsistent.
description: Standardized Valeriana officinalis extract (often valerenic-acid rich) supports CNS-relevant inhibitory neurotransmission, aligning with conservative sleep-quality research framing.
safetyNotes: Generally well tolerated for most users.
contraindications: Concurrent benzodiazepines or opioids or alcohol or other sedative or CNS depressant medication due to additive sedation risk
existing liver disease such as chronic hepatitis or fatty liver disease due to isolated case reports of reversible liver enzyme elevation with high-dose or long-term use
discontinue at least two weeks before any procedure requiring general anesthesia due to additive sedative effects
concurrent medications metabolized by CYP3A4 due to valerian's potential to reduce their clearance and increase drug levels
pregnancy or breastfeeding due to insufficient safety data.
